title: artemisia ludoviciana subsp. lindleyana
intro: Artemisia ludoviciana subsp. lindleyana, also known as Western mugwort, is a perennial herb native to western North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and aiding in the relief of menstrual cramps. Western mugwort can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
